next_steps: Lyme disease serology (ELISA followed by Western Blot)
Cardiac MRI with gadolinium enhancement to assess for inflammation or fibrosis
Echocardiogram to evaluate for structural abnormalities or wall motion abnormalities
24-hour Holter monitoring to characterize conduction disturbances
Initiation of intravenous ceftriaxone if Lyme serology is suggestive or clinical suspicion remains high
Telemetry monitoring for high-degree AV block
Consultation with infectious disease and cardiology
